title: Slate Auto launches a radically simple electric pickup truck from $24,950
summary: The vehicle lacks a radio and electric windows to achieve a disruptive price and minimalist design.

title: China unveils the Lineshine supercomputer, the most powerful machine in the world
summary: The system reaches 2,198 exaflops on the TOP500 list, surpassing the United States at the top of high-performance computing.

title: Anthropic launches Claude Tag to integrate its AI into Slack
summary: The tool already writes 65% of companies' internal code and promises to transform collaboration from instant messaging.

title: RAM prices skyrocket in the second quarter of 2026
summary: LPDDR5X modules rise 89% and SSDs 54%, as the industry awaits Micron's results to gauge demand.

title: GTA VI captures attention amid leaks and official pricing of its editions
summary: Rockstar confirms the game's editions and warns about scams, while the physical version will come with a download code instead of a disc.

title: Meta launches $299 smart glasses without the Ray-Ban brand
summary: The new device seeks to cut costs by removing the association with Luxottica to democratize augmented reality.

title: Meta develops its own prediction market with the Arena app
summary: Mark Zuckerberg's initiative forgoes monetary bets to integrate a forecasting system into the company's ecosystem.

title: LastPass suffers a new data breach due to a supply chain attack on Klue
summary: Hackers stole customer service information by exploiting a credential compromised since 2022.

title: Mistral launches its OCR 4 model with multilingual document extraction
summary: The new AI tool outperforms competitors in 72% of test cases.

title: Valve expands SteamOS to all PC hardware and launches new Steam Machines
summary: The company works with Intel, AMD, and Nvidia to make its system universal, with a console from $1,049.

title: Trump orders migration to post-quantum cryptography before 2030
summary: An executive order sets imminent deadlines for the federal government to update its systems in the face of the quantum threat.

title: United States blocks Mythos AI for violating classified systems
summary: The AI discovered a massive memory leak undetected since the Clinton era, prompting drastic national security measures.

title: The race for AI chips accelerates with new alliances and multi-billion dollar acquisitions
summary: OpenAI presents its own chip with Broadcom, and Qualcomm acquires startup Modular for nearly $4 billion.

title: FUTO Swipe presents an open-source predictive swipe keyboard
summary: The new tool aims to offer a modern and efficient alternative for typing on mobile devices.

title: Europe tightens the noose on Chinese digital commerce and distrust of Big Tech
summary: A new flat tariff for small packages and the loss of trust in US companies mark the regulatory shift.

title: Massive sell-off of artificial intelligence stocks shakes global markets
summary: Fear of a bubble in the sector causes falls on Wall Street and drags down Asian markets.

title: Official White House app automatically downloads on government phones and cannot be uninstalled
summary: Federal workers denounce that the software injects 'pure propaganda' while authorities ignore privacy complaints.

title: SpaceX raises $25 billion in debt sale after IPO
summary: Elon Musk's company obtains massive funding to boost its space exploration and communications plans.

title: Five Eyes alliance warns of imminent use of AI in massive cyberattacks
summary: Intelligence services warn that AI-powered attacks will occur within months, overwhelming governments and companies.

title: Sweden hampers Tesla's plans to launch autonomous driving in Europe
summary: Swedish justice complicates Elon Musk's ambitious plans to implement the Full Self-Driving system across the continent.
